rationale: Rebuilt commit 2b55d1a7 to /tmp/mindspec-spec110-bead2-g1-r2 using offline caches and reran the corrected R5 oracle. For all 11 round-1 flipped specs, branch `validate spec` rejects and branch `validate plan` rejects with the identical sorted `impacted-domains-resolve` message set, confirming the check moved earlier rather than introducing a new rejection class. I also verified `plan.go` has zero reviewed diff. The ADR digit-boundary fix is empirically good: targeted Go subtests pass, the full ADR touchpoint extraction test passes, and CLI fixtures for `[ADR-12345](...)` and `[ADR-00311](...)` with ADR-0031 present both exit 0 with no `adr-touchpoint-missing`. Focused `go test ./internal/validate ./internal/approve -count=1` passes. No new issue found.

findings.round1_title: ValidateSpec introduces pass-to-fail regressions for existing specs
findings.evidence: Under the corrected R5 oracle, all 11 flipped specs fail `validate plan` with the identical `impacted-domains-resolve` diagnostics already produced by new `validate spec`; impacted counts were 2,1,2,1,1,1,1,3,5,1,2 for specs 038,051,060,071,072,073,080,083,084,085,090 respectively.

findings.round1_title: Five-digit ADR touchpoint IDs are truncated to four digits
findings.evidence: The reviewed regex requires a structural non-digit boundary after ADR-####. Targeted subtests and CLI fixtures show `[ADR-12345](...)` no longer reports missing ADR-1234, and `[ADR-00311](...)` does not resolve through existing ADR-0031 or emit a new error.
